title: Calendar (world level)
description: Genre-appropriate timekeeping for a specific world: day names, month structure, seasons, festivals, and time precision. Lets the narrator say 'dawn on Restday in Thawmelt' instead of 'it's morning'. PRD §2.3. Lives at sidequest-content/genre_packs/<pack>/worlds/<world>/calendar.yaml.

name.description: Optional. In-world name for the calendar system itself (e.g. 'The Turning', 'Imperial Standard').

days_in_year.description: Total days in a full year. Sum of month lengths SHOULD equal this; generator may warn if they diverge.

day_names.description: Ordered list of weekday names. Length MUST equal `days_in_week`.

months.description: Ordered list of months. The cycle repeats yearly.

moons.description: Optional. Celestial bodies with cycle metadata. Used by mechanics that care about moon phase (lycanthropy, tides, ritual magic).

time_precision.description: Granularity at which the narrator tracks time of day. Closed enum: the prompt zone format depends on the choice.

epoch.description: Optional starting date for a fresh campaign in this world. If absent, sessions begin at the world's authoring default.

name.description: Month name as the narrator says it.

season.description: Season id this month belongs to. MUST match a season id used in the pack's weather.yaml climate zones — otherwise the prompt zone cannot align weather to month.

festivals.description: Optional festival list. Each entry is a short phrase the narrator may surface as a hook (e.g. 'First Planting — seeds blessed by clergy'). Free-form prose, but keep one entry to one sentence.

cycle_days.description: Full cycle length, dawn-of-new to dawn-of-new.

months.name: April
festivals: Easter — village fete on the green

months.name: August
festivals: Glenross Show — annual fair, prize livestock and homemade jam
